headline: The heartland is outrunning the prime districts, with suburban and city-fringe homes still climbing while the most expensive addresses in the core give ground.

board.l: Private median psf, +3.8% on the year
HDB median psf, +0.3% on the year
HDB gross yield, versus 3.6% on private

wei: Private resale is near $1,753 psf, +3.8% on the year. The market has slowed, not turned.
The suburbs lead at $1,523 psf (+1.4%), while prime has eased to $2,203 psf (-2.4%).
New launches clear around $2,361 psf, well above the $1,523 psf you would pay resale nearby.
hana: HDB rents throw off about 6.1% gross, versus 3.6% on private. For cash yield, HDB still wins.
Rents have come off the boil: private near $4,300 a month, HDB near $3,200.
hot: Parktown Residence is the busiest launch, clearing near $2,361 psf.
Marine Parade HDB is the biggest mover, +11.0% over three months.
Chuan Park went en bloc for $890.00m.

persona.buyer: Hunt in the OCR suburbs near the $1,523 psf resale benchmark where momentum is strongest, and do not overpay in prime CCR while it is still sliding 2.4%.
persona.seller: List into strength now, with private resale up 3.8% on the year and HDB towns like Marine Parade and Toa Payoh firming, before the MOP supply wave broadens buyers' choices.
persona.landlord: Favour HDB for its 6.1% yield over private's 3.6%, and price to fill quickly because rents have come off the boil and empty months cost more than a lower rent.
persona.upgrader: Sell the flat into a firm market and buy your private replacement on the value tiers, but line up the timing so you are not caught paying the 20% second-home ABSD.

board.title: June 2026: the market now
board.label: Private median psf
board.say: Private resale prices are flattening out. The median now sits at $1,753 psf, up just 0.6% over the month and 3.8% over the year, with the typical deal closing at about $1.65m. Across the past 18 months the market has logged 22,196 transactions, so this is a broad reading, not a thin one swinging on a handful of sales. The latest month is still preliminary, but the headline is calm.

prices.title: Where prices are moving
prices.label: Suburbs (OCR) three-month momentum
prices.say: Private resale prices are flattening out. The median now sits at $1,753 psf, up just 0.6% over the month and 3.8% over the year, with the typical deal closing at about $1.65m. Across the past 18 months the market has logged 22,196 transactions, so this is a broad reading, not a thin one swinging on a handful of sales. The latest month is still preliminary, but the headline is calm.

rental.title: Rents off the boil, HDB the yield play
rental.label: HDB gross yield, versus 3.6% private
rental.say: The heat has come out of the rental market. Private homes now let for a median $4,300 a month, and at today's prices that works out to a gross yield of 3.6%. After the surge of the past few years, tenants have some footing back: more completed units, more choice, and less willingness to chase asking rents higher. Landlords who bought at recent prices are finding the sums tighter, and holding out for a top rent increasingly means holding out for an empty flat.

hot.title: What is moving this month
hot.label: Parktown Residence, top-selling launch psf
hot.say: New launches are selling at prices that make resale look cheap. In the suburbs, where existing homes trade around $1,523 psf, buyers are paying $2,113 psf at Tengah Garden Residences, $2,169 at Springleaf Residence, $2,361 at Parktown Residence and $2,598 at Chuan Park. Move to the city fringe and the numbers climb again, to $2,723 psf at The Orie and $2,957 at One Marina Gardens. These are the best sellers, so this is not a few overpriced units sitting unsold.
